description: A client has legitimately added a community mirror source M and bound M's index-signing key in config (MKT-061). M serves a kind: pack entry claiming the acme namespace, but acme never signed it: the entry's publisher_signature does not chain, through channel-index/1 CHI-012's root-signed publisher-namespace delegation, to the key acme's namespace is delegated. channel-index/1 CHI-050 passes (M's index is validly signed by M's own configured channel key) and the digest/size verify, yet the per-publisher check MKT-009 fails -- the entry is refused as PUBLISHER_SIGNATURE_UNVERIFIED, proving a source's own index signature never establishes entitlement to a namespace it was never delegated. An otherwise-identical entry whose publisher_signature chains to acme's delegated key would be accepted.
